vision: A mobile app that helps individuals manage their daily tasks through simple task creation, completion tracking, and organisation without the complexity of full project management tools.

epics.name: Task Creation & Management
epics.description: Core functionality enabling users to create, edit, and delete tasks to maintain their to-do list

stories.title: Create a new task with title
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to create a new task with a title, so that I can capture things I need to do
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the task list screen When I tap the add task button and enter a title and save Then a new task appears in my task list
Given I am creating a new task When I leave the title empty and try to save Then the app prevents saving and shows a validation message

stories.title: Add optional due date to task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to add an optional due date when creating a task, so that I can track when tasks need to be completed
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a new task When I tap the due date field Then a date picker is displayed
Given I am creating a new task When I skip the due date field and save Then the task is created without a due date

stories.title: Edit an existing task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to edit an existing task, so that I can correct mistakes or update task details
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an existing task When I tap on the task to edit and modify the title and save Then the task displays the updated title
Given I am editing a task When I change the due date and save Then the task displays the updated due date

stories.title: Delete a task from the list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to delete a task from my list, so that I can remove tasks that are no longer relevant
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an existing task When I select delete on that task Then the task is removed from my task list
Given I am about to delete a task When I confirm the deletion Then the task is permanently removed

epics.name: Task Completion Tracking
epics.description: Functionality to mark tasks as complete and track progress through the to-do list

stories.title: Mark a task as complete
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to mark a task as complete, so that I can track my progress and see what I've accomplished
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an incomplete task When I tap the complete button or checkbox Then the task is visually marked as complete
Given I have marked a task complete When I view my task list Then the completed task shows a distinct completed state

stories.title: Unmark a completed task as incomplete
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to unmark a completed task, so that I can restore it to my active list if marked complete by mistake
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a completed task When I tap the complete toggle Then the task returns to incomplete state
Given I have unmarked a task When I view my task list Then the task appears as an active incomplete task

epics.name: Task List Viewing
epics.description: Display and viewing capabilities for users to see their tasks in an organised list format

stories.title: View all tasks in list format
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to view all my tasks in a list format, so that I can see everything I need to do at a glance
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks saved When I open the app Then I see all my tasks displayed in a list
Given I have no tasks When I open the app Then I see an empty state message indicating no tasks

stories.title: Filter to show only incomplete tasks
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my task list to show only incomplete tasks, so that I can focus on what still needs to be done
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I select the incomplete filter Then only incomplete tasks are displayed
Given I am viewing filtered incomplete tasks When I have no incomplete tasks Then I see an empty state message

stories.title: Filter to show only completed tasks
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my task list to show only completed tasks, so that I can review what I have accomplished
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I select the completed filter Then only completed tasks are displayed
Given I am viewing filtered completed tasks When I have no completed tasks Then I see an empty state message

stories.title: Sort tasks by due date
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to sort my tasks by due date, so that I can see upcoming deadlines first and prioritise accordingly
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks with due dates When I select sort by due date Then tasks are ordered with earliest due dates first
Given I have tasks with and without due dates When I sort by due date Then tasks without due dates appear at the end of the list

epics.name: Data Persistence
epics.description: Local storage functionality to ensure tasks are saved and persist between app sessions

stories.title: Persist tasks locally on device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want my tasks to be saved locally on my device, so that I don't lose my task list when I close the app
acceptance_criteria: Given I have created tasks When I close and reopen the app Then all my previously created tasks are still visible
Given I have marked tasks as complete When I close and reopen the app Then the completion status is preserved
stories.notes: Walking skeleton - essential for any functional app

stories.title: Load task list within one second
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want my task list to load within one second, so that I can quickly access my tasks without waiting
acceptance_criteria: Given I have up to 100 tasks stored When I open the app Then the task list is fully displayed within 1 second
Given I am viewing my task list When I apply a filter Then the filtered results display within 1 second

epics.name: Cross-Platform Support
epics.description: Ensure the app is available and functions correctly on both iOS and Android mobile platforms

stories.title: Use app on iOS device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my iOS device, so that I can manage tasks on my iPhone or iPad
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an iOS device When I download and install the app Then the app launches and functions correctly
Given I am using the app on iOS When I perform all task operations Then they work according to iOS platform conventions

stories.title: Use app on Android device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my Android device, so that I can manage tasks on my Android phone or tablet
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an Android device When I download and install the app Then the app launches and functions correctly
Given I am using the app on Android When I perform all task operations Then they work according to Android platform conventions
